title: Repo Profile (`.claude/repo-profile.yaml`) Schema
description: Validates the canonical `.claude/repo-profile.yaml` written by `.claude/scripts/detect-repo-profile.py` (PLAN-083 Wave 0a sub-agent 0.6). This file pins the repo's risk class for smart-loading, trading guardrails, first-run banner, and the kill-switch fail-CLOSED ceremony. Per PLAN-083 R1 Codex P0: unknown structure MUST NOT silently fall through to `generic`; the detector either emits `risk_class: trading-readonly` (fail-CLOSED default) or `risk_class: unknown-needs-owner-confirmation` (when ambiguity is high enough to demand explicit Owner ack via `detect-repo-profile.py confirm-profile <name>`). The 7-day read-only banner consumer (sub-agent 2.7) keys off `created_at`. The trading-readonly hook deny-list consumer (sub-agent 2.7) keys off `manual_review_paths`.

risk_class.description: The detected (or Owner-confirmed) repo profile. Drives smart-loading per-profile caps (frontend <=10, engine <=12, fintech <=15, trading-readonly <=8, generic <=6) and trading guardrails. Per PLAN-083 §7.1 fail-CLOSED requirement, the detector defaults to `trading-readonly` when signals are ambiguous, and to `unknown-needs-owner-confirmation` when no signals fire at all (caller must run `confirm-profile <name>` to proceed). `generic` is reachable ONLY via explicit Owner `confirm-profile generic` (never via auto-detect).

detected_at.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 UTC timestamp (e.g. `2026-05-11T15:42:01Z`) of the most recent detection run. Updated on every `detect-repo-profile.py detect`. Distinct from `created_at` which is fixed at first-run.

confidence.description: Confidence in the detection result. `high` = >=3 strong signals all agreeing; `medium` = 1-2 strong signals OR multiple weak signals agreeing; `low` = ambiguous, mixed, or fail-CLOSED default. Sub-agent 2.1 first-run wizard SHOULD surface a confirmation prompt when confidence != `high`.

signals.description: Ordered list of heuristic signals that fired during detection. Each entry is a short slug describing the matched evidence (e.g. `package.json:next`, `pyproject.toml:fastapi`, `dir:strategies/`, `file:exchanges/binance.py`, `dir:compliance/`, `fallback:no-signals`). Empty signals array combined with `risk_class: unknown-needs-owner-confirmation` indicates the fail-CLOSED path. Audit forensics consumes this field.

manual_override.description: True when the current `risk_class` was set via `detect-repo-profile.py confirm-profile <name>` (explicit Owner ACK) rather than auto-detection. Once true, subsequent `detect` runs MUST NOT silently overwrite the file; they re-detect and refuse to mutate, requiring `confirm-profile` again. This satisfies the §7.5 kill-switch contract: the file is the trust anchor, not the detection algorithm.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the FIRST successful detection or confirmation. Fixed for the lifetime of this `.claude/repo-profile.yaml`. Consumed by sub-agent 2.7 to render the 7-day read-only banner (banner remains visible until `now() - created_at > 7d`). Resetting the file via `rm` + re-detect deliberately resets this clock — that is part of the kill-switch fail-CLOSED escape-hatch ceremony per §7.5.

manual_review_paths.description: Optional deny-list of file globs/paths that the trading-readonly hook (sub-agent 2.7) MUST block on Edit/Write attempts. Pre-populated by the detector when `risk_class: trading-readonly` is set (default >=10 fixtures per PLAN-083 CR NTH). Owner may extend post-detection; the file is owned-sentinel-scope for `.claude/repo-profile.yaml` only (NOT canonical-guard kernel).

schema_version.description: Schema version pin. Bumped only when this schema's required-fields or enum membership changes in a backward-incompatible way. Sub-agent 2.7 / 2.1 consumers MUST refuse to operate on a `repo-profile.yaml` whose `schema_version` they do not recognize.

notes.description: Optional free-form Owner annotation. Persisted across `detect` runs. Not interpreted by any consumer; purely human-readable.
